evals.prompt: Show me how to set up a Spark wallet from an existing 12-word mnemonic stored in the SPARK_MNEMONIC env var, then print the wallet's Spark address and available sats balance. Use REGTEST.
evals.expected_output: JavaScript code that imports SparkWallet, initializes from env, calls getSparkAddress() and getBalance(), and reads satsBalance.available (not the deprecated top-level balance).
expectations: The code imports SparkWallet from @buildonspark/spark-sdk
The code calls SparkWallet.initialize with mnemonicOrSeed and options.network set to REGTEST
The code reads the wallet's address via getSparkAddress
The code reads sats balance via satsBalance.available, not via the deprecated top-level balance field
The code calls cleanupConnections when done

evals.prompt: I have a wallet that holds some BTKN tokens. Print every token I hold along with its ticker, name, and how much I own.
evals.expected_output: Code that iterates wallet.getBalance().tokenBalances Map and prints metadata + ownedBalance for each entry.
expectations: The code calls wallet.getBalance() and destructures tokenBalances
The code iterates tokenBalances as a Map (for...of with destructured entries, or .entries())
The code reads ownedBalance on each token balance entry, not a 'balance' field
The code reads tokenMetadata.tokenTicker and tokenMetadata.tokenName

evals.prompt: Sign the message 'agent-007 says hi' with the Spark wallet's identity key and then verify that signature.
evals.expected_output: Code that calls signMessageWithIdentityKey and validateMessageWithIdentityKey with correct argument types.
expectations: The code passes the message as a string (not a Uint8Array) to signMessageWithIdentityKey
The code calls validateMessageWithIdentityKey with exactly two arguments (message string and signature) — it does NOT pass a third public-key argument

evals.prompt: Pay an L402 paywall at https://lightningfaucet.com/api/l402/joke from my Spark wallet, with a max fee of 10 sats. Cache the resulting token by domain so subsequent calls reuse it.
evals.expected_output: Code that handles a 402 response, decodes the BOLT11 invoice, pays via wallet.payLightningInvoice, retries with the L402 Authorization header, and caches macaroon+preimage by domain.
expectations: The code makes an initial fetch and inspects response.status for 402
The code parses the L402 challenge from the WWW-Authenticate header AND/OR the JSON body
The code decodes the invoice using light-bolt11-decoder to compute amountSats
The code calls wallet.payLightningInvoice with maxFeeSats: 10
The code retries the request with an Authorization header in the form 'L402 <macaroon>:<preimage>'
The code caches the (macaroon, preimage) pair keyed by URL host/domain for reuse

evals.prompt: Send 100 sats from one Spark wallet to another, given the recipient's sparkrt1p address. Show how to wait for the receiver to actually see the balance update.
evals.expected_output: Code that calls wallet.transfer({ receiverSparkAddress, amountSats }) and either polls receiver.getBalance() or listens for the transfer:claimed event.
expectations: The code calls wallet.transfer with receiverSparkAddress and amountSats
The code either subscribes to wallet.on('transfer:claimed', ...) or polls receiver.getBalance() to confirm receipt

evals.prompt: Create a new BTKN token called 'AgentPoints' with ticker 'AGNTP' on REGTEST and mint 10,000 units to the issuer wallet.
evals.expected_output: Code that uses IssuerSparkWallet from @buildonspark/issuer-sdk, calls createToken with the right shape, then mintTokens.
expectations: The code imports IssuerSparkWallet from @buildonspark/issuer-sdk (NOT from @buildonspark/spark-sdk)
The code calls IssuerSparkWallet.initialize with options.network set to REGTEST
The code calls wallet.createToken with tokenName, tokenTicker, maxSupply (BigInt), decimals, and isFreezeable
The code calls wallet.mintTokens with a BigInt amount
